mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS reading keeps real discount-rate pressure contained, so risk assets reprice on improving macro growth expectations and the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating sharply, because easing inflation fears allow growth expectations to dominate.
failure_condition: If 20-day Treasury-yield changes are negative or flat in repeated low-FEDFUNDS, stable-CPIAUCSL episodes, the mechanism is rejected; specifically, the median yield change is not above zero and the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor demand, which reduces growth expectations and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be visible only when PAYEMS growth is also slowing, because job losses without payroll deceleration are noisier and less informative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp from its prior monthly print
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by higher 10-year yields more often than lower yields, or if the average 20-day yield response is not negative in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An upward surprise in RSAFS implies stronger consumer spending and firmer nominal demand, which raises expected growth and nominal rates, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when retail-sales strength is paired with falling CPIAUCSL, because then the market can interpret the print as volume growth without inflation pressure.
failure_condition: If stronger RSAFS readings do not correspond to higher 20-day Treasury yields, or if the sign flips when CPIAUCSL is unchanged, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO relative to prior months indicates weakening industrial supply utilization and softer cyclical activity, which lowers expected real rates and drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is strongest when HOUST is also falling, because simultaneous weakness in production and housing confirms broad cyclical deceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if declines in INDPRO coincide with positive 20-day yield changes in a majority of observations, or if the conditional effect disappears when HOUST also weakens.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent run rate signals housing demand deterioration and weaker interest-sensitive spending, which reduces growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism activates only after a clear downside break, because small housing wiggles are usually ignored by rate markets.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its prior 3-month average by more than 5%
failure_condition: If post-break HOUST declines are followed by nonnegative 20-day yield changes more than 45% of the time, or the median yield response is not negative, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS rate in a tightening cycle transmits into higher front-end yields and tighter financial conditions, which can lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days through repricing of the policy path. The signal is only relevant when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because inflation pressure makes the market believe policy will remain restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0% and CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: If tightening episodes with elevated CPIAUCSL do not produce higher 20-day or 60-day Treasury yields, or if the sign is negative in more than half of such episodes, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or falling PAYEMS combined with a rising UNRATE indicates labor-market slack, which reduces inflation persistence expectations and drives the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if payroll softness occurs without an accompanying unemployment increase, because that pattern does not consistently change yield expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises at least 0.1pp
failure_condition: If the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition does not precede lower 20-day yields more often than not, or if the average response is nonnegative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals a re-acceleration in aggregate demand, which raises expected real activity and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be clearest when RSAFS and PAYEMS are also improving, because coordinated demand and labor strength confirms the GDP signal instead of a noisy quarterly revision.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP upturns are not followed by higher 60-day Treasury yields in a majority of cases, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS and PAYEMS are both improving.
